text.title: cognitive-inclusion-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cognitive-inclusion-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: "Dyslexia-friendly fonts" (e.g., OpenDyslexic) have weak empirical support—provide as option, not default.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Empirical evidence for dyslexia fonts as universal defaults is weak; offer as a user-controlled option rather than imposing on users who may find it harder to read.

failure-mode.description: Customization (font, color, spacing) only helps if discoverable and persistent across sessions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A hidden or session-reset setting provides zero benefit; implementation is as critical as the design pattern itself.

failure-mode.description: Plain-language and "literal language" can clash with branded voice; product needs a tone-of-voice exception list.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cognitive design patterns assume content is already written in plain language; restructuring a screen cannot fix fundamentally complex or jargon-heavy source text.

failure-mode.description: Reducing motion globally can break critical animations (focus indicators, progress feedback).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Indiscriminate motion removal breaks critical feedback signals; scope carefully and test with users relying on animation for navigation.

failure-mode.description: Time-limit removal can collide with security session timeouts—needs UX + security joint review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Some time limits exist for security reasons; unilateral removal creates vulnerabilities that require coordinated design.

failure-mode.description: Most automated tools cannot detect cognitive issues; requires structured manual review + user research.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cognitive accessibility requires human evaluation. Automated tools cover only syntactic checks (headings, labels); they cannot evaluate reading level, mental model alignment, or interaction flow clarity.

failure-mode.description: Agents add jargon to "sound professional"—enforce a banned-word list (utilize → use, leverage → use, etc.).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM default tone is formal and jargon-heavy. Provide a banned-word list and require ≤20 words/sentence + ≤2 ideas per sentence.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to long sentences; require max 20 words/sentence and ≤2 ideas per sentence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cognitive inclusion requires short, simple sentences. Explicit constraints prevent complex sentence structures.

failure-mode.description: Agents apply OpenDyslexic globally—keep it opt-in; default font choice is contested.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OpenDyslexic empirical support is weak. Treat as user-optional, not default.

failure-mode.description: "Reduce motion" agent adds * { animation: none } blanket rule, breaking focus indicators—scope carefully.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Indiscriminate motion removal breaks critical UX. Audit which animations are essential (focus, progress) before suppressing.

failure-mode.description: Time-limit removal vs. security session: agent must surface this as a trade-off, not silently extend.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Some time limits are security-critical. Agent must alert humans to this conflict instead of unilaterally removing the limit.

failure-mode.description: Agents recommend overlay widgets—refuse; document fix at source.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overlay widgets cannot fix structural issues and may worsen accessibility.

failure-mode.description: Cognitive needs are heterogeneous; avoid grouping users by diagnosis. Design for needs (focus, time, structure), not labels.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two autistic users may have completely different needs. Design for underlying needs (structure, literal language) rather than diagnostic labels.
